title: EIP-7911: Scaling Ethereum with a Perceptron Tree ZKP
description: Proposes a Perceptron Tree-based zero-knowledge proof model to enhance Ethereum scalability and privacy. The hybrid model combines decision trees and perceptron neural networks to compress multiple transaction relationships into a single proof, reducing gas fees and on-chain verification load compared to existing zk-SNARK and zk-STARK methods.
author: Ryo Ha (@sopia19910) <sopia19910@gmail.com>, Khajiev Nizomjon (@khajievN) <nizom7812@gmail.com>
laymanDescription: Uses a machine-learning-inspired tree structure to group related transactions and prove them all at once with a single zero-knowledge proof. This reduces gas costs and verification work on-chain while keeping transaction details private. No protocol upgrade is required; it works at the smart contract level.

endUsers.description: Users interacting with contracts that adopt this model could see lower gas fees for batched transaction verification.

appDevs.description: Developers building ZKP-based applications could integrate the Perceptron Tree verifier contracts for more efficient multi-transaction proofs.

walletDevs.description: No direct wallet changes required; operates at the smart contract layer with standard transaction interfaces.

toolingInfra.description: ZKP tooling and analytics platforms may need to support the new Perceptron Tree proof format for debugging and monitoring.

layer2s.description: Rollups and L2s using ZKP verification could adopt the model to compress proof submissions and reduce L1 settlement costs.

clClients.description: No consensus client changes needed; the proposal operates entirely at the smart contract level without protocol modifications.

elClients.description: No execution client changes needed; verification occurs within smart contracts using existing EVM capabilities.

benefits: Compresses multiple transaction proofs into one, reducing gas costs for batch verification.
Preserves transaction privacy while proving validity and inter-transaction relationships.
Requires no Ethereum protocol changes; deployable as smart contracts on the existing network.
Addresses limitations of zk-SNARK and zk-STARK by leveraging inter-transaction similarity.
tradeoffs: Introduces complexity from the Perceptron Tree training and commitment computation steps.
Proof generation depends on off-chain tree construction, adding an external trust assumption.

scaleL1.description: Reduces on-chain verification load by consolidating multiple transaction proofs into a single compact proof.
